petitioner_background: The petitioner is a musician/songwriter from Serbia (based on references to Serbian music festivals and organizations) who sought classification as an alien of extraordinary ability under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act. The record references the petitioner's involvement with the International Music Festival MESAM, the Moravski Biseri Festival, SABOR magazine, and a list of songs by the petitioner. The Nebraska Service Center Director denied the petition on March 27, 2008, and the AAO dismissed the petitioner's appeal on March 31, 2009. The matter came before the AAO on a motion to reopen and a motion to reconsider.
outcome_reasoning: The AAO dismissed both the motion to reopen and the motion to reconsider. The motion to reopen was dismissed because none of the evidence submitted qualified as 'new' under 8 C.F.R. § 103.5(a)(2) — all documents were previously available and could have been discovered or presented in the prior proceeding, and the petitioner had already been put on notice of evidentiary requirements through the director's December 19, 2007 request for additional evidence. The AAO emphasized that a motion to reopen is not an opportunity for counsel to correct her own defects in the record, such as insufficient translations. The motion to reconsider was dismissed because counsel merely reiterated the same arguments from the original appeal and failed to support the motion with any precedent decisions establishing that the prior decision was based on an incorrect application of law or USCIS policy. The petitioner failed to meet the 'heavy burden' required for reopening, and the burden of proof remained with the petitioner under section 291 of the Act.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: This decision addresses procedural issues related to motions to reopen and reconsider rather than the substantive extraordinary ability criteria. The AAO found that counsel attempted to use the motion to reopen to cure her own prior defects in the record (specifically insufficient translations of previously submitted articles) and to submit evidence that had originally been requested by the director's December 19, 2007 RFE but not timely provided. The AAO cited Matter of Soriano to emphasize that the petitioner had been put on notice of evidentiary requirements and had a reasonable opportunity to provide the required evidence. Counsel also improperly used the motion to reconsider to re-argue the same points raised on appeal without citing any pertinent precedent decisions showing legal or policy error.
